Calculated geometric altitude of Sun at Anchorage on Nov 16 using Astropy/Skyfield. The Sun crosses -6 degrees at 02:13 UTC. The claimed 02:10 corresponds to 5:10 PM local time for Nov 16.

Calculated geometric altitude of Sun at Anchorage on Nov 16 using Astropy/Skyfield. The Sun crosses -6 degrees at 17:18 UTC. The claimed 17:08 contradicts astronomy tools and falls outside the verifier's 5-minute tolerance. It appears to mistakenly derive from Nov 17 evening twilight (5:08 PM).

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real astronomical coordinates for Anchorage International Airport (PANC). The requested target times for civil twilight (Sun altitude = -6° geometric) on November 16, 2024, are incorrect. Code analysis via Astropy and Skyfield demonstrates that evening civil twilight crosses at 02:13 UTC (contradicting 02:10), and morning civil twilight crosses at 17:18 UTC (contradicting 17:08). A critical bug exists because 17:18 falls completely outside the verifier's allowed +/- 5 minute window for the morning check, rendering a perfect score practically impossible if the agent correctly extracts the true data from Stellarium.
